content: We can reframe curiosity-driven autonomous agent design to treat long-term memory itself as the active learning query function, where intrinsic curiosity signals from ICM/RND mechanisms prioritize which unlabeled real-world environmental experiences get encoded into the agent’s working long-term memory, rather than using active learning only to select external training data. This creates a self-augmenting cycle where the agent’s growing memory shapes its future curiosity, which in turn improves its memory storage organization.
